Title: Abscisic acid modulates neighbor proximity-induced leaf hyponasty in Arabidopsis

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Short-chain dehydrogenase/reductase involved in ABA biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates leaf position/hyponasty and water-related physiology
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: aba2 mutants have low ABA, constitutively more erect leaves in white light, and a severely impaired LRFR-induced hyponastic response
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotyping under white light and LRFR; comparison of leaf elevation angles; auxin application response assays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ABA2 is required for normal ABA accumulation capacity supporting leaf positioning. 2. aba2 mutants phenocopy nced3 nced5 in showing erect leaves in white light. 3. aba2 mutants respond weakly to LRFR and to exogenous IAA-induced hyponasty, indicating ABA acts downstream of LRFR-induced auxin production.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low red/far-red ratio (LRFR); white light; exogenous IAA
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Smaller rosettes in strong ABA-defective mutants noted generally
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although ABA2 is part of ABA biosynthesis, this paper does not present direct mechanistic experiments on ABA2 expression or enzyme activity; evidence is genetic/phenotypic.

Common_Genes.Gene_Name: PYR1; PYL1; PYL2; PYL4; PYL5; PYL8
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Modulates leaf positioning and ABA responsiveness
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher-order ABA receptor mutants, especially pyr1pyl1pyl2pyl4pyl5pyl8, show constitutively erect leaves and reduced LRFR-induced hyponasty
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Higher-order mutant phenotyping under white light and LRFR; amplitude analysis of leaf movement
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The sextuple receptor mutant has constitutively more erect leaves in white light. 2. ABA receptor function is required for a robust LRFR-induced hyponastic response. 3. The quadruple mutant shows little phenotype, indicating functional redundancy among ABA receptors.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong ABA signaling mutants display smaller rosettes
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper also reports LRFR-associated transcriptional downregulation of several positively acting RCAR/PYR1/PYL genes in petioles.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Clade A PP2C protein phosphatases; ABA co-receptor negative regulators
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Context-dependent control of leaf movement and ABA responses
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher-order pp2c mutants alter diel leaf movement patterns and reduce LRFR responsiveness; dominant-negative abi1-1 expression in specific tissues compromises LRFR hyponasty and ABA-induced leaf repositioning
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Higher-order mutant phenotyping; tissue-specific dominant-negative abi1-1 transgenic lines driven by CAB3, CYP86A2, MYB76, and SCR promoters; ABA spray assays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PP2C family members modulate normal leaf movement behavior and LRFR-induced hyponasty. 2. Tissue-specific interference with ABI1-mediated ABA signaling in stomata, mesophyll, and bundle sheath reduces LRFR-induced hyponasty. 3. ABA signaling is required in multiple leaf tissues for ABA-induced downward repositioning in white light.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low red/far-red ratio (LRFR); white light; exogenous ABA
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CAB3 constitutive/mesophyll-associated; CYP86A2 cell-type-specific; MYB76 cell-type-specific; SCR cell-type-specific
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong ABA signaling perturbation is associated with altered growth and abnormal diel movement patterns
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: LRFR weakly induces several ABI1-family negative regulators at the transcript level. abi1-1 is used as a dominant-negative tool rather than a native allele regulating metabolism directly.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Required for ABA signal transduction controlling leaf angle responses
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: snrk2.2 snrk2.3 snrk2.6 triple mutants have constitutively erect leaves and no LRFR response
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Triple mutant phenotyping under white light and LRFR; comparison with single and double mutants; high-humidity growth rescue control
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SnRK2.2/2.3/2.6 act redundantly as essential positive ABA signaling components for hyponasty. 2. Only the triple mutant shows a dramatic defect, indicating strong redundancy. 3. The reduced LRFR response is not explained solely by smaller plant size because humidity-rescued larger triple mutants remain defective.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low red/far-red ratio (LRFR); white light; saturating humidity
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Severe growth defects and smaller rosettes in the triple mutant
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Several SnRK2 genes are transcriptionally reduced under LRFR according to re-analysis of expression data.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Epidermal patterning factors controlling stomatal density
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Indirectly linked to water balance and leaf positioning via stomatal density/transpiration
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: epf1epf2 mutants have more erect leaves in white light but retain robust LRFR-induced hyponasty; EPF2 overexpression causes altered but present leaf movements
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phenotyping of epf1epf2 and EPF2-OX lines under white light then LRFR
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Altered stomatal density affects baseline leaf angle in white light. 2. Normal LRFR-induced hyponasty can still occur despite altered stomatal density and transpiration baseline. 3. Stomatal density is therefore not a major limiting control point for LRFR-induced hyponasty.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because experimentally analyzed in Results, but these genes do not directly modulate ABA metabolism.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Oxidative cleavage step from epoxycarotenoids to xanthoxin precursor branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative cleavage of 9-cis-epoxycarotenoids to produce xanthoxin, a precursor of abscisic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms a direct precursor in the ABA biosynthetic pathway and controls ABA production rate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Established rate-limiting enzyme for ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls leaf hyponasty and water-related physiology under WL and LRFR
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves/rosettes, including petioles and leaf blades
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: LRFR rapidly induces NCED3 expression and promotes ABA accumulation; loss of NCED3 together with NCED5 abolishes LRFR-induced ABA increase and strongly reduces LRFR hyponasty
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR time course in petiole and lamina; ABA quantification; nced3 nced5 double mutant analysis; ChIP-qPCR showing PIF4 binding to promoter under LRFR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NCED3 is rapidly induced by LRFR, especially in petioles and also in leaf blades. 2. PIF4 binds the NCED3 promoter more strongly after LRFR, supporting direct transcriptional control. 3. NCED3 together with NCED5 is required for LRFR-induced ABA accumulation and full hyponastic response.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Rapidly induced by LRFR in petiole and leaf blade
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Rapid LRFR induction in leaves, strongest in petioles
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function in combination with nced5 leads to constitutively erect leaves and reduced LRFR responsiveness
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct PIF4 promoter binding was experimentally shown; NCED3 expression is reduced in pif4 pif5 pif7 even in WL.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Oxidative cleavage step from epoxycarotenoids to xanthoxin precursor branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative cleavage of 9-cis-epoxycarotenoids to produce xanthoxin, contributing to abscisic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms a direct precursor in the ABA biosynthetic pathway and contributes to ABA production rate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Established rate-limiting enzyme for ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls leaf hyponasty and water-related physiology under WL and LRFR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: NCED5 is induced by LRFR in petioles and acts with NCED3 to enable ABA accumulation and robust LRFR hyponasty
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis from genome-wide data and RT-qPCR; nced3 nced5 double mutant ABA quantification and hyponasty phenotyping; single-mutant comparisons
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NCED5 is rapidly induced by LRFR in petioles. 2. NCED5 contributes redundantly with NCED3 to LRFR-induced ABA accumulation. 3. Single mutants show mild phenotypes, while the double mutant shows strong ABA and hyponasty defects.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Rapidly induced by LRFR in petiole
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Combined loss with NCED3 causes reduced ABA and impaired LRFR response
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct promoter binding by PIF4 was shown for NCED3, not explicitly for NCED5 in this study.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional activation of ABA biosynthesis under shade conditions
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Low red/far-red ratio (neighbor proximity shade signal); phytochrome signaling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct LRFR-responsive activator linking shade perception to ABA production
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Modulates leaf hyponasty and water-related physiology
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PIF4 is required for LRFR-induced NCED3 expression and ABA accumulation, thereby promoting normal LRFR-induced leaf hyponasty
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: pif4 pif5 pif7 mutant ABA quantification; RT-qPCR of NCED3; ChIP-qPCR of PIF4-HA on NCED3 promoter under LRFR
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LRFR increases PIF4 binding to the NCED3 promoter. 2. PIF4 is required, together with PIF5 and PIF7, for LRFR-induced NCED3 expression and ABA accumulation. 3. PIF4 directly connects shade signaling to ABA biosynthesis in leaves.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to tune shade avoidance and hormone balance, but likely pleiotropic because PIF4 also regulates auxin and growth pathways
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely broad pleiotropic effects on shade avoidance and growth architecture
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PIF4 was experimentally shown to bind NCED3 promoter regions corresponding to known peaks; HFR1 used as positive control target.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome-wide expression data from Fiorucci et al., 2022 reanalyzed in this study; accession NA

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Promotion of ABA biosynthesis under shade conditions
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation, inferred from triple-mutant requirement
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Low red/far-red ratio (neighbor proximity shade signal)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes LRFR-induced NCED expression together with PIF4/PIF7
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Required as part of the PIF module for LRFR-induced ABA increase
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of PIF5 together with PIF4 and PIF7 abolishes LRFR-induced ABA increase
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ABA quantification and NCED3 expression analysis in pif4 pif5 pif7 triple mutant
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PIF5 is part of the regulatory module required for ABA accumulation in LRFR. 2. Triple pif4 pif5 pif7 mutants fail to induce NCED3 and ABA after LRFR transfer. 3. PIF5 contributes to linking shade signaling with ABA-mediated hyponasty.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for modifying shade responses, but broad architectural effects are likely
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely pleiotropic effects on shade avoidance traits
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct binding assay for PIF5 was shown in this study; role is inferred from triple mutant.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Promotion of ABA biosynthesis under shade conditions
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation, inferred from triple-mutant requirement
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Low red/far-red ratio (neighbor proximity shade signal)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes LRFR-induced NCED expression together with PIF4/PIF5
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Required as part of the PIF module that enables ABA production under LRFR
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of PIF7 together with PIF4 and PIF5 abolishes LRFR-induced ABA increase
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ABA quantification and NCED3 expression analysis in pif4 pif5 pif7 triple mutant
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PIF7 is part of the PIF module required for LRFR-triggered ABA biosynthesis. 2. Triple pif4 pif5 pif7 mutants fail to accumulate ABA in LRFR. 3. PIF7 contributes to the signaling cascade connecting shade cues to hyponastic growth.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential node for tuning shade responses, with expected pleiotropy across light-regulated growth
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely broad shade-avoidance and growth tradeoffs
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct binding was not separately demonstrated here; contribution inferred from the pif4 pif5 pif7 triple mutant.
